dialogue.greeting: The first light always finds a way through, little Grovekeeper. You did not come this far by accident.
dialogue.firstMeet: I am Dawnfall — I was here before the first seed split its shell. Welcome to the waking grove. Every tree you have ever planted cast its shadow across my watching eyes.
subsequent: Each morning the forest holds its breath, then exhales with you. I notice.
You return. Good. The grove grows restless without its keeper.
Even the oldest oak began as you did — uncertain, small, reaching.

reward.lore: Dawnfall shaped the first meadow clearings so that light could teach seeds which way was up. She still tends the horizon every morning, just out of sight.

dialogue.greeting: Ah — a wanderer who does not fear the fading light. Most folk rush home before now.
dialogue.firstMeet: I am Eventide. I watch over the grove when the day releases its hold. The creatures that come out at dusk? They are mine. The silence between the last birdsong and the first cricket? Also mine.
subsequent: Even the hardiest tree rests its sap at nightfall. You should learn from that.
You have grown roots here, Grovekeeper. I can feel them under my feet.
The grove looks different in this light, does it not? Softer. Honest.

reward.lore: Eventide was woven from the last warmth of the world's first sunset. He lingers at forest edges so that nothing must face the dark alone.

dialogue.greeting: You come in the rain without complaint. Already you understand more than most.
dialogue.firstMeet: Soakhollow is what they call me — though I prefer the sound of water finding its own level. I fill the hollow places. I feed the roots that cannot see the sky.
subsequent: The river does not hurry. It arrives when it arrives. So do you.
Every drought you have weathered made the next rain sweeter. I remember each one.
Your grove drinks deep. I am pleased with what you have built here.

reward.lore: Soakhollow carved the first stream beds with a single patient season of rain. She still rides every storm cloud over the grove, checking that nothing thirsts.

dialogue.greeting: Still? Never. But for you, I will slow to a whisper — just for a moment.
dialogue.firstMeet: Rustlepass carries what cannot walk. Seeds, spores, the last warmth before a cold snap. I am why trees grow in places no one planted them. I am the original Grovekeeper, in a way.
subsequent: I scattered three of your seeds further than you know. They found good soil.
A grove too sheltered never grows strong. Bend with me, and you learn balance.
The canopy makes a different sound when it trusts the wind. Yours does now.

reward.lore: Rustlepass mapped every air current above the world in her first restless century. She still finds new ones, and delights in every discovery.

dialogue.greeting: You tapped a tree once. I felt it. No harm done — you knew to stop when the tree spoke.
dialogue.firstMeet: I run through every trunk you have ever touched, Grovekeeper. Veindeep is what the old trees named me. The slow rivers inside living wood — that is where I live.
subsequent: Patience is not waiting. It is moving at exactly the right speed.
The tree you planted first — its sap has almost reached the crown now. Well done.
Some things cannot be hurried. The grove knows this. You are learning it.

reward.lore: Veindeep taught the first trees to move water upward against gravity — a thing that should be impossible and still is, technically. She considers this her finest trick.

dialogue.greeting: You noticed me. Most people look at the rock and never see what is growing on it.
dialogue.firstMeet: Greenmantl is who I am, and I am very old. I have been recording the history of this grove since before there was a grove — the spores remember everything. I am glad you came.
subsequent: The old trees speak slowly. Give them time and they say beautiful things.
I have covered stranger stones than your worries. Everything can be grown over.
You are part of the grove's memory now. I will keep it well.

reward.lore: Greenmantl wrote the grove's entire history in the pattern of his own growth across the oldest stones. Scholars tried to read it once. They needed three lifetimes and were still only through the prologue.

dialogue.greeting: Oh! You are here just in time — everything is opening at once and I cannot decide where to look first!
dialogue.firstMeet: Petalwarden, at your service! I am in charge of all the best moments — the first blossom of spring, the smell before rain, the way canopies catch the afternoon light. A pleasure to meet you properly!
subsequent: Your cherry tree bloomed beautifully this spring. I made sure of it.
Joy is not loud. Sometimes it is just a grove full of things growing well.
Come back in spring. I have plans for this place that I think you will love.

reward.lore: Petalwarden negotiated the treaty between the pollinators and the flowers in the world's second spring, when both sides were still figuring out the arrangement. She still oversees the relationship with great pride.

dialogue.greeting: You have tended well. I do not say that to everyone. Only to those who have earned it.
dialogue.firstMeet: I am Yieldkeeper — last of the eight, guardian of the completed thing. The others tend beginnings and middles. I tend endings that become beginnings again. You have found all of us. That means the grove is ready.
subsequent: The harvest is not the end. It is the seed for next season.
What you have built here will outlast you. That is the highest compliment I know.
The Worldroot stirs. I think it has been waiting for a keeper like you.

reward.lore: Yieldkeeper wove the first autumn so that trees would know how to let go gracefully. She considers the act of dropping leaves — trusting the cycle — the bravest thing any living thing can do.
